zh.lifeSummary: William Travisse是William Travisse和Mrs. William Travisse的兒子，於1596年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾維根。他和Rebecca Booth至少有7個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1664年9月22日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州享年68歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。

nameMeanings.name: Travisse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Travis
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire and Yorkshire): from Old French and Middle English travers, also Middle English travas, travis, a word with several meanings. It may denote a toll paid for the right to cross a boundary or bridge, the surname perhaps being for someone who paid or collected such tolls. Alternatively, it may mean ‘crosswise, sideways’ or ‘dispute, disagreement’. The Old French word also had the sense ‘crossing, passage’, and it is more than likely that the surname is Norman in origin, for someone who lived at a crossing place or at a place so named (compare French Travers 3). The presence of the preposition ‘de’ (and perhaps also ‘le’) in early forms of the surname appears to indicate a topographic or habitational explanation, but the French preposition and definite article were sometimes added gratuitously to Norman surnames as a sign of their high social status. See also Travers 1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Travisse was born in 1596, in Wigan, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William Travisse and Mrs. William Travisse. He had at least 7 sons and 2 daughters with Rebecca Booth. He died on 22 September 1664, in Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Virginia, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: William Travisse(은)는 1596년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 위건에서 William Travisse(와)과 Mrs. William Travisse의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Rebecca Booth(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1664년 9월 22일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: William Travisse родился в 1596 г. в местности виган, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали William Travisse и Mrs. William Travisse. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери от Rebecca Booth. Он умер 22 сентября 1664 г. в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.
